text.title: FinOps — Spot / Preemptible Instances for Fault-Tolerant Workloads Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: ML training job runs on spot for 12 hours; interruption at hour 11 wipes all progress; team runs it on-demand thereafter.
antipattern.root-cause: Author moved a long-running, uncheckpointed job to spot.
antipattern.fix: Add checkpointing every 5 min (model snapshot to S3); on resume, load latest checkpoint; verify checkpoint restore works under interruption.

antipattern.symptom: Spot fleet 100% m5.large; AWS reclaims m5 in the region; fleet drops to zero for 4 hours.
antipattern.fix: Diversify across m5, m6i, m6a, c5, c6i in ≥ 2 AZs; let EC2 Fleet pick lowest-priced capacity available.

antipattern.symptom: Service runs 100% spot; large reclamation event zeros the fleet; on-demand fallback never provisioned; full outage 30 min.
antipattern.root-cause: Author bet 100% on spot to maximize savings.
antipattern.fix: Provision a 10-20% on-demand floor in the fleet; spot is the cheap upper layer, on-demand is the survival floor.
